gold.rationale: The article's central claim is fabricated: the cited rumor-control notice states that reports of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda being 'fully dismantled and repaired on the ground' are false, and that current work consists of deformation monitoring, comprehensive surveying, information-model building, and partial reinforcement. The visuals further mislead by using synthetic and edited-looking scenes to make the nonexistent dismantling plan appear real.

atomic_points.post_summary: Chinese authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level restoration of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda in Shanxi province to address structural issues, a decision that has sparked debate among experts and concerns among tourists.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Chinese cultural heritage authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda in northern Shanxi province, setting in motion a rare intervention on one of the world’s oldest and tallest surviving timber towers after years of structural assessment and debate over how best to preserve it.
atoms: Chinese cultural heritage authorities approved a full dismantling of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda.
Chinese cultural heritage authorities approved a ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is located in northern Shanxi province.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is one of the world's oldest surviving timber towers.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is one of the world's tallest surviving timber towers.
The intervention on the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is described as rare.
The decision followed years of structural assessment.
The decision followed debate over how best to preserve the pagoda.

sentence_level.sentence_text: According to cultural heritage planning materials reviewed by people familiar with the project, the decision marks a turn away from earlier reinforcement-led efforts toward a complete dismantling-and-repair operation.
atoms: Cultural heritage planning materials were reviewed by people familiar with the project.
The decision marks a turn away from earlier reinforcement-led efforts.
The decision marks a turn toward a complete dismantling-and-repair operation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The aim is to deal with long-term deformation, accumulated structural stress and what specialists see as the limits of piecemeal stabilization.
atoms: The aim of the overhaul is to deal with long-term deformation.
The aim of the overhaul is to deal with accumulated structural stress.
Specialists see limits to piecemeal stabilization.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Preservation experts involved in consultations said the plan envisions taking the pagoda down section by section, restoring it at ground level and then reassembling it after damaged or weakened members are treated or replaced under strict conservation protocols.
atoms: Preservation experts involved in consultations described the plan.
The plan envisions taking the pagoda down section by section.
The plan envisions restoring the pagoda at ground level.
The plan envisions reassembling the pagoda after restoration.
Damaged or weakened members will be treated or replaced.
Treatment or replacement will occur under strict conservation protocols.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Several people with knowledge of the process said local officials in Yingxian and figures in provincial heritage circles have been preparing for the move for months.
atoms: Local officials in Yingxian have been preparing for the move for months.
Figures in provincial heritage circles have been preparing for the move for months.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That followed surveys, digital mapping and engineering reviews that concluded a full disassembly offered the safest long-term path for protecting the monument.
atoms: Surveys were conducted on the pagoda.
Digital mapping was conducted on the pagoda.
Engineering reviews were conducted on the pagoda.
Reviews concluded that full disassembly offers the safest long-term path for protecting the monument.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The project has not yet been fully opened to public view, but site preparations, access controls and materials planning are understood to be under way before a formal construction phase begins.
atoms: The project has not yet been fully opened to public view.
Site preparations are understood to be under way.
Access controls are understood to be under way.
Materials planning is understood to be under way.
These activities are occurring before a formal construction phase begins.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, also known as the Sakyamuni Pagoda of Fogong Temple, was built in 1056 during the Liao dynasty and is widely regarded as a masterpiece of Chinese timber architecture.
atoms: The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is also known as the Sakyamuni Pagoda of Fogong Temple.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda was built in 1056.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda was built during the Liao dynasty.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is widely regarded as a masterpiece of Chinese timber architecture.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Standing about 67 meters high, it has long attracted architects, historians and tourists because of its sophisticated bracket systems, layered internal structure and unusual survival through centuries of earthquakes, warfare and exposure to the elements.
atoms: The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda stands about 67 meters high.
The pagoda has attracted architects, historians, and tourists.
The pagoda has sophisticated bracket systems.
The pagoda has a layered internal structure.
The pagoda survived centuries of earthquakes.
The pagoda survived centuries of warfare.
The pagoda survived centuries of exposure to the elements.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Specialists say its importance extends beyond China, describing it as a singular reference point for the study of large-scale premodern wooden construction.
atoms: Specialists say the pagoda's importance extends beyond China.
Specialists describe the pagoda as a singular reference point for the study of large-scale premodern wooden construction.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That standing has only intensified concern over the building’s condition.
atoms: The pagoda's status has intensified concern over its condition.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Researchers and conservation planners have spent years recording tilt, localized distortion, stress in key load-bearing members and the aging of timber connections across several levels.
atoms: Researchers and conservation planners have spent years recording the pagoda's condition.
Recorded issues include tilt.
Recorded issues include localized distortion.
Recorded issues include stress in key load-bearing members.
Recorded issues include aging of timber connections across several levels.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Monitoring campaigns using laser scanning, total-station survey equipment, photogrammetry and high-resolution digital modeling have created a detailed record of minute changes in the structure.
atoms: Monitoring campaigns used laser scanning.
Monitoring campaigns used total-station survey equipment.
Monitoring campaigns used photogrammetry.
Monitoring campaigns used high-resolution digital modeling.
These campaigns created a detailed record of minute changes in the structure.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Experts involved in the assessment said those studies, together with on-site inspections and historical analysis of earlier repairs, formed the basis for the decision to dismantle it.
atoms: Experts involved in the assessment said studies formed the basis for the decision to dismantle.
On-site inspections formed the basis for the decision to dismantle.
Historical analysis of earlier repairs formed the basis for the decision to dismantle.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Under the current technical plan, the overhaul is expected to unfold in carefully sequenced stages.
atoms: The overhaul is expected to unfold in carefully sequenced stages.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Workers would begin by installing external protective systems and temporary supports.
atoms: Workers will begin by installing external protective systems.
Workers will begin by installing temporary supports.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Architectural elements and structural members would then be removed in an order designed to preserve load paths and historic fabric.
atoms: Architectural elements and structural members will be removed.
Removal order is designed to preserve load paths.
Removal order is designed to preserve historic fabric.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Each timber component is to be photographed, measured, tagged and cataloged before being moved to ground-level conservation zones, where specialists will check for cracking, compression, biological deterioration and traces of past repairs.
atoms: Each timber component is to be photographed.
Each timber component is to be measured.
Each timber component is to be tagged.
Each timber component is to be cataloged.
Components will be moved to ground-level conservation zones.
Specialists will check for cracking.
Specialists will check for compression.
Specialists will check for biological deterioration.
Specialists will check for traces of past repairs.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Once restoration is complete, the pagoda would be reassembled, with its original spatial logic, joinery sequence and material hierarchy reproduced as closely as possible.
atoms: The pagoda will be reassembled once restoration is complete.
Original spatial logic will be reproduced as closely as possible.
Original joinery sequence will be reproduced as closely as possible.
Original material hierarchy will be reproduced as closely as possible.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Preparations for the project also include a digital inventory linking each component to three-dimensional models generated during recent mapping campaigns.
atoms: Preparations include a digital inventory.
The digital inventory links each component to three-dimensional models.
Three-dimensional models were generated during recent mapping campaigns.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Specialists said that database is expected to guide both dismantling and reassembly by allowing conservators to track the exact position, condition and treatment history of individual beams, columns, bracket arms and connecting elements.
atoms: The database is expected to guide dismantling.
The database is expected to guide reassembly.
The database allows tracking of exact position of components.
The database allows tracking of condition of components.
The database allows tracking of treatment history of components.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They said the method reflects a broader trend in heritage engineering, in which detailed information models are used to support interventions on highly complex historic structures.
atoms: The method reflects a broader trend in heritage engineering.
Detailed information models are used to support interventions on highly complex historic structures.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Cover image: A wide view of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda shows cranes and scaffold towers positioned around the upper tiers as preservation teams prepare for the staged dismantling approved under the overhaul plan.
atoms: Cranes are positioned around the upper tiers of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda.
Scaffold towers are positioned around the upper tiers of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda.
Preservation teams are preparing for staged dismantling.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Mid-article image: Barriers, stacked timber sections and suspended beams appear near the base of the pagoda during early site organization for the ground-level restoration phase.
atoms: Barriers appear near the base of the pagoda.
Stacked timber sections appear near the base of the pagoda.
Suspended beams appear near the base of the pagoda.
These are present during early site organization for ground-level restoration.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The scale of the operation has stirred debate among conservation professionals, architects and regular visitors to the site.
atoms: The scale of the operation has stirred debate among conservation professionals.
The scale of the operation has stirred debate among architects.
The scale of the operation has stirred debate among regular visitors.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Some specialists argue that only a full dismantling can address structural problems that have built up over decades and lower the risk of further deformation from seasonal loads and visitor pressure.
atoms: Some specialists argue only full dismantling can address structural problems.
Structural problems have built up over decades.
Full dismantling can lower the risk of further deformation from seasonal loads.
Full dismantling can lower the risk of further deformation from visitor pressure.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Others warn that taking apart a monument of such age and complexity carries risks of its own, including the possible loss of historical integrity if too many elements need intervention during treatment and reassembly.
atoms: Others warn that taking apart the monument carries risks.
Risks include possible loss of historical integrity.
Loss of integrity may occur if too many elements need intervention during treatment and reassembly.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Tourists and local businesses are watching the process closely as well.
atoms: Tourists are watching the process closely.
Local businesses are watching the process closely.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The pagoda is one of Shanxi’s best-known heritage attractions and a major draw on domestic travel routes centered on ancient architecture.
atoms: The pagoda is one of Shanxi’s best-known heritage attractions.
The pagoda is a major draw on domestic travel routes centered on ancient architecture.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Visitors at the site this week said they understood the need for preservation work, but hoped access restrictions would be handled in a way that still allowed limited viewing or interpretation exhibits during the closure period.
atoms: Visitors at the site this week said they understood the need for preservation work.
Visitors hoped access restrictions would allow limited viewing during closure.
Visitors hoped access restrictions would allow interpretation exhibits during closure.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Officials and planners expect the dismantling phase to start after final site mobilization and protective works are finished.
atoms: Officials and planners expect the dismantling phase to start after final site mobilization.
Officials and planners expect the dismantling phase to start after protective works are finished.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Given the size of the tower and the precision the work will require, the overall project is likely to last several years.
atoms: The overall project is likely to last several years.

sentence_level.sentence_text: During that time, the monument is expected to face temporary closure or sharply reduced access, while surrounding areas come under tighter management as restoration teams move equipment, process timber components and prepare for the eventual reassembly of the 11th-century landmark.
atoms: The monument is expected to face temporary closure or sharply reduced access.
Surrounding areas will come under tighter management.
Restoration teams will move equipment.
Restoration teams will process timber components.
Restoration teams will prepare for the eventual reassembly of the 11th-century landmark.

paragraph_level: Chinese authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, shifting from previous reinforcement strategies.
The decision is based on years of structural assessments, digital mapping, and engineering reviews indicating that piecemeal stabilization is insufficient.
The restoration plan involves section-by-section dismantling, ground-level treatment of components, and precise reassembly using digital inventories.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, built in 1056 during the Liao dynasty, is a 67-meter-tall masterpiece of Chinese timber architecture.
Structural concerns include long-term deformation, tilt, stress in load-bearing members, and aging timber connections.
The project utilizes advanced monitoring technologies like laser scanning and photogrammetry to track structural changes.
The dismantling and restoration process is expected to take several years and involve temporary closure or reduced access.
The decision has sparked debate among experts regarding the risks of dismantling versus the necessity of addressing structural decay.
Local businesses and tourists are concerned about access restrictions during the multi-year restoration period.

retrieved_evidence.text: In recent reports about the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda in Shanxi, protective scaffolding, cranes and safety barriers have been described around the multi‑storey timber structure, with workers and heritage technicians appearing to prepare for more intensive conservation measures. The Yingxian pagoda, dating back to the Liao dynasty and often cited as one of China’s oldest surviving all‑wooden towers, has long been monitored for structural deformation, leaning and weathering. According to local cultural relics authorities, emergency reinforcement zones and controlled access areas are common preliminary steps when major preservation or potential dismantling work is being considered for such nationally protected monuments.

According to accounts circulating among some local observers in recent days, heritage authorities are said to have opted for a comprehensive dismantling-and-repair campaign for the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, rather than limiting work to partial reinforcement. People familiar with internal discussions suggest that the proposed approach would involve intervening across the entire structure in a coordinated conservation programme. Officials have not released detailed timetables, and they emphasise that any extensive work would be subject to further expert consultation and approval procedures at higher levels.

Technical accounts relating to the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda indicate that any dismantling for conservation would require meticulous engineering preparation near the base and along the upper tiers. Descriptions mention suspended timber segments, temporary working platforms, numbered braces and heavy engineering equipment positioned around load‑bearing points, suggesting a cataloguing of components and stress redistribution before any element is removed. According to cultural heritage engineers familiar with wooden pagodas in Shanxi, each beam, bracket arm and column would typically be tagged and documented so that later reassembly can follow the original structural logic and satisfy national relic protection standards.

The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda in Shanxi province is being highlighted by heritage experts in recent reports as a landmark timber structure dating back to the 11th century, according to officials with the local cultural relics authority. The multi-storey Buddhist tower, built entirely of wood, is regarded as an important symbol of traditional Chinese architecture and religious history in northern China. Researchers note that the monument embodies sophisticated structural techniques from the Liao period and has been repeatedly cited in academic studies as a key reference point for the study of ancient wooden construction in East Asia.

Preservation specialists and academic observers have in recent days discussed possible approaches to safeguarding the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, with particular attention to whether dismantling would be the safest long-term option, according to people following the debate. Some experts cited in local media are described as favouring a comprehensive dismantling-and-reassembly strategy to address hidden structural risks, while others argue for more limited reinforcement measures in order to minimise intervention in the original fabric. Participants say the discussion is ongoing and that no single view has yet gained universal support.

Local authorities in Shanxi province are said to be considering temporary adjustments to visitor access at the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda in connection with anticipated conservation work, according to tourism officials. Under the scenarios currently being discussed, the site could be fully closed or subject to stricter entry controls for a certain period, in order to ensure safety and to facilitate technical operations. Travel agencies have been informally advised that arrangements may change at short notice once a final schedule for the project is confirmed.

Archival descriptions of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, also known as the Sakyamuni Pagoda of Fogong Temple, highlight its status as one of China’s most important historic wooden monuments well before any recent restoration plans. Travel accounts and conservation surveys portray a tall octagonal structure rising above the surrounding county town, with multiple eaves, intricate bracket sets and Buddhist statues housed inside. Over the past decades, experts from China’s State Administration of Cultural Heritage have repeatedly expressed concern about wind damage, seismic vulnerability and accumulated tilting, arguing that the centuries‑old timber system requires careful long‑term intervention to prevent irreversible loss.

Early descriptions of a proposed conservation project for the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda suggest that it would be carried out in successive stages, according to individuals briefed on preliminary plans. The scheme is said to involve dismantling the timber structure section by section, moving each part to ground level for restoration work, and later reassembling the tower once repairs are completed. People familiar with the discussions stress that such an operation would require meticulous documentation, labelling and handling of individual components, and that the technical details remain under study.

Heritage conservation practice in China, as described by specialists working on sites like the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, places strong emphasis on pre‑dismantling documentation of each structural member. Accounts of preparatory work refer to close inspection of timber joints, digital tagging of individual beams and columns, and the creation of detailed three‑dimensional records before any major intervention proceeds. According to conservation professionals, such work is intended to record information about original carpentry techniques, deformation patterns and hidden decay, thereby guiding later reinforcement or reconstruction efforts while complying with national and UNESCO‑inspired principles on the treatment of ancient wooden architecture.

【网传“应县木塔即将全部拆卸落地大修”不实】近期，有自媒体发文称“应县木塔即将全部拆卸落地（架）大修”，引发网民议论。对此，中国互联网联合辟谣平台向有关主管部门核实，确认相关信息系谣言。 应县木塔始建于辽代，是我国现存最高、体量最大、年代最古老的木结构楼阁式塔，是全国重点文物保护单位。目前，有关方面正在开展木塔变形监测、全面测绘与信息模型搭建，推进局部加固工作。网络上出现“应县木塔即将全部拆卸落地大修”信息均为谣言。 中国互联网联合辟谣平台提醒广大网民，关于应县木塔的保护与修缮信息，请以国家文物局、山西省文物局及当地官方渠道发布的消息为准，切勿轻信和传播谣言。（来源：中国互联网联合辟谣平台）

Cultural heritage specialists in Shanxi have recently reiterated that the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is facing long-term preservation pressures, according to information released by local authorities. Experts involved in routine monitoring say that the ancient timber structure shows signs of gradual deformation and ongoing structural stress, issues they describe as not uncommon for large-scale wooden monuments of this age. The situation is said to require sustained conservation efforts, including careful assessment of load-bearing components and measures to mitigate the impact of environmental changes over the coming years.

The reported conservation plan for the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is being linked by some experts to a series of recent surveys, digital mapping efforts and structural assessments, according to cultural heritage researchers. Over the past few years, specialised teams have conducted on-site measurements, three-dimensional scanning and material analysis aimed at building a detailed profile of the monument’s condition. In some accounts, these activities are presented as steps leading towards a possible dismantling project, although specialist circles note that such data are routinely gathered to inform a range of preservation options.

Cover image: A wide view of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda shows cranes and scaffold towers positioned around the upper tiers as preservation teams prepare for the staged dismantling approved under the overhaul plan.
Mid-article image: Barriers, stacked timber sections and suspended beams appear near the base of the pagoda during early site organization for the ground-level restoration phase.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post asserts that authorities have 'approved' a full dismantling plan, whereas the official fact-check (news_025_6) explicitly labels this claim as a rumor and states that only local reinforcement and monitoring are currently underway.
text_analysis.t1_evidence: The post claims 'Chinese cultural heritage authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul', but evidence news_025_6 explicitly states that reports of 'immediate full dismantling and ground-level major repair' are rumors and that the current official work is limited to 'deformation monitoring, comprehensive mapping... and local reinforcement'.
t1_problematic_subclaims: Chinese cultural heritage authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post frames the dismantling as a decided 'turn away from earlier reinforcement-led efforts' with 'site preparations... under way', whereas evidence news_025_6 and news_025_3 indicate that dismantling is merely a subject of debate or rumor, and the actual approved action is 'local reinforcement' and 'monitoring', not a full dismantling project in execution.
t2_problematic_subclaims: The decision marks a turn away from earlier reinforcement-led efforts toward a complete dismantling-and-repair operation
site preparations, access controls and materials planning are understood to be under way
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post presents a debunked rumor as an approved official fact, significantly misrepresenting the current status of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda's conservation efforts.

images.scene_description: A wide shot of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda under construction, featuring extensive scaffolding on the lower tiers and two large cranes (one yellow, one green) flanking the structure. In the foreground, workers in hard hats are sorting through stacks of timber beams.

image_analysis.global_notes: All four images are realistic, candid photographs that accurately depict the scenes described in the post text and evidence. There are no signs of AI synthesis, digital editing, or semantic inconsistencies.

judge.level3_rationale: 该帖子的核心主张被官方辟谣平台明确证实为谣言，且部分配图存在明显的AI生成特征（如图片2中建筑上的乱码牌匾），因此判定为虚假。
judge.reasoning_trace: The core claim of 'approved full dismantling' is directly contradicted by factcheck_025. The text presents this fabrication as fact. The images are real but are used to support a false narrative about the type of work being done. T1 and T2 fire. C1 fires because the text claims dismantling while the visual context (and reality) is reinforcement. 3 L2 labels + T1 + Contradicted core claim -> Mostly False.
judge.core_claim: Chinese authorities have officially approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda, and site preparations are underway.
key_subclaims: Chinese cultural heritage authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda.
The decision marks a turn away from earlier reinforcement-led efforts toward a complete dismantling-and-repair operation.
Site preparations, access controls and materials planning are understood to be under way before a formal construction phase begins.
The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is one of the world’s oldest and tallest surviving timber towers.
The project involves taking the pagoda down section by section, restoring it at ground level and then reassembling it.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Chinese cultural heritage authorities have approved a full dismantling and ground-level overhaul of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda.
subclaim_findings.note: The official fact-check explicitly states that reports of 'immediate full dismantling' are rumors and false. Authorities are currently conducting monitoring and local reinforcement, not full dismantling.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The decision marks a turn away from earlier reinforcement-led efforts toward a complete dismantling-and-repair operation.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms that local reinforcement work is being advanced, contradicting the claim that a decision has been made to switch to full dismantling.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Site preparations, access controls and materials planning are understood to be under way before a formal construction phase begins.
subclaim_findings.note: While some monitoring and reinforcement preparations are real, the text frames them as preparations for a 'full dismantling' which is false. The images show scaffolding which is consistent with reinforcement, not necessarily dismantling.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The Yingxian Wooden Pagoda is one of the world’s oldest and tallest surviving timber towers.
subclaim_findings.note: Historical facts about the pagoda's age and status are accurate.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The project involves taking the pagoda down section by section, restoring it at ground level and then reassembling it.
subclaim_findings.note: The official rebuttal denies that such a project has been approved or is imminent.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post's core claim—that authorities have approved a full dismantling of the Yingxian Wooden Pagoda—is explicitly contradicted by an official fact-check (factcheck_025) which labels it a rumor. The text fabricates the approval status (T1) and distorts the nature of the ongoing work (T2), claiming it is for dismantling when it is actually for reinforcement/monitoring. The images show scaffolding which supports the 'work is happening' aspect but the text's interpretation of that work is false, creating a semantic inconsistency (C1) between the visual evidence of reinforcement and the textual claim of dismantling. With T1 firing and multiple contradicted subclaims, the verdict is Mostly False.

image_findings.explanation: The image shows the pagoda with scaffolding and cranes, which is visually consistent with the text's description of 'site preparations', though the text misinterprets the nature of the work (dismantling vs reinforcement).
The image shows scaffolding and a 'restoration site' sign, consistent with the text's claim of ongoing work, but the text falsely claims this is for full dismantling.
Aerial view of the pagoda, consistent with general context.
Workers inspecting the structure with tags, consistent with the text's description of monitoring and documentation.
